title: Chisocheton longistipitatus
taxonomy: Chisocheton longistipitatus is classified in the family Rutaceae. It belongs to the genus Chisocheton, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Asia. Chisocheton longistipitatus is an evergreen tree or shrub found in the forests of Southeast Asia. The classification of Chisocheton longistipitatus at the order level is in the order Sapindales, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the soapberry order. Chisocheton longistipitatus is further classified at the kingdom level as a part of the Plantae kingdom.
uses: Chisocheton longistipitatus, a plant native to Borneo, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. Its leaves and bark are reportedly used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant's extracts are also believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for medicinal use.
